title: Ordered CatBoost with raw high-cardinality categorical interactions
summary: Train/test contain hundreds of drivers and race-year context combinations; CatBoost was built to handle categorical leakage via ordered statistics and often wins when manual frequency encodings plateau.
rationale: Train/test contain hundreds of drivers and race-year context combinations; CatBoost was built to handle categorical leakage via ordered statistics and often wins when manual frequency encodings plateau. This is meaningfully different from the current mostly numeric-threshold feature stack.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoostClassifier on mostly raw columns plus a few causal numeric features, enabling ordered boosting; explicitly try interactions like `Driver x Race`, `Driver x Compound`, `Race x Compound`, `Year x Compound`, and compare against the current GBM using purged OOF.
expected_effect: Can recover sparse strategy priors and driver-track behavior without fragile handcrafted target encoders.
risk: If validation is weak, CatBoost can look unrealistically strong; also may underperform if the true signal is mostly continuous and already captured.
